provenance.source_corpus: Growing season carbon gas exchange from peatlands used as a source of vegetation donor material for restoration

source_spans.text: The moss layer transfer technique removes the top layer of vegetation from donor sites

nodes.name: removal of the top layer of vegetation

source_spans.text: The moss layer transfer technique removes the top layer of vegetation from donor sites
This is due to removal of the top layer of vegetation in the donor sites that results in a reduced depth to the water table
Methane flux was related to water table position at both sites, with the flux increasing with shallower water table
Methane flux increased with increasing sedge cover at the bog site
decreased with increasing shrub cover ... at the fen site
decreased with increasing ... moss cover ... at the fen site
decreased with increasing ... total cover ... at the fen site
Higher sedge cover resulted in more negative NEE max values
GEPmax became more negative (greater CO2 uptake) in plots with higher moss cover across the bog sites
ER increased as GEPmax became more negative
greater CO2 uptake through NEE max with ... higher soil temperature at 5 cm

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:17e8ccf2a386
edges.original_sentence: The moss layer transfer technique removes the top layer of vegetation from donor sites as a method to transfer propagules and restore degraded or reclaimed peatlands.

source_spans.text: The moss layer transfer technique removes the top layer of vegetation from donor sites as a method to transfer propagules and restore degraded or reclaimed peatlands.
